_note: Regex patterns for PR comment classification. Pre-compiled at import time by pr.py for fetch-comments, triage, and triage-batch subcommands. Classification priority: code_change(high) > code_change(medium) > code_change(low) > ignore > explain. Priority vocabulary uses PRIORITY_LEVELS (low, medium, high, critical) from triage_helpers.

_category_rationale.code_change.high: Security and correctness issues that demand immediate code changes — bugs, crashes, vulnerabilities.
_category_rationale.code_change.medium: Actionable requests using directive language (please add/fix/change) or calling out specific incorrectness.
_category_rationale.code_change.low: Style and cosmetic suggestions — typos, naming, formatting. Lower urgency but still actionable.
_category_rationale.explain.low: Questions and clarification requests — reviewer wants understanding, not necessarily a code change.
_category_rationale.ignore.low: Pure acknowledgments (LGTM, approved) and bot comments — no human action needed.

thresholds._rationale: substantial_comment_length (100): below this, comments without keyword matches are typically drive-by acknowledgments. context_match_min_length (20): below this, comments are too short to contain meaningful code references for context matching. camel_case_min_length (4): minimum word length for camelCase identifier detection in context matching.
